criteria: Fork a child process using fork(); child calls PTRACE_TRACEME then execvp() the target program
Parent uses PTRACE_SYSCALL + waitpid(-1) loop to stop all traced processes at each syscall boundary
Entry/exit state tracked per-PID with toggle flag: entry reads syscall number from orig_rax, exit reads return value from rax
Syscall names decoded from x86_64 syscall table indexed by syscall number
String arguments read from tracee memory word-by-word via PTRACE_PEEKDATA with errno=0 pre-clear and -1L+errno!=0 error detection
Bitmask flags decoded symbolically for open() (O_RDONLY|O_CREAT|...) and mmap() (PROT_READ|MAP_PRIVATE|...)
strace-style output format: syscall_name(arg1, arg2) = return_value
Error returns in [-4095,-1] displayed as -1 ERRNO (description)
Signal delivery stops re-inject signal number via PTRACE_SYSCALL last argument
PTRACE_O_TRACEFORK, PTRACE_O_TRACEVFORK, PTRACE_O_TRACECLONE set before first PTRACE_SYSCALL resume
PTRACE_O_TRACESYSGOOD enabled; syscall stops identified as SIGTRAP|0x80 (0x85), not plain SIGTRAP
waitpid(-1) loop dispatches events from any traced process by PID
PTRACE_EVENT_FORK/VFORK/CLONE handled: PTRACE_GETEVENTMSG retrieves child PID; new pid_state_t created; PTRACE_SETOPTIONS set on child; child resumed with PTRACE_SYSCALL
PTRACE_EVENT_EXEC handled: in_syscall set to 1, outbuf_len reset to 0, current syscall_info cleared
Per-PID state stored in 64-slot open-addressing hash map (pid_state_t) with Fibonacci hashing and linear probing
num_active counter tracks live traced processes; main loop exits when num_active reaches 0
All output lines tagged with [pid N] prefix and built into per-PID outbuf for atomic fputs emission
clock_gettime(CLOCK_MONOTONIC) called at entry stop (entry_time) and exit stop; timespec_diff_ns computes elapsed_ns clamped to 0
stats_record() accumulates call_count, error_count, total_ns into g_stats[400] at every exit stop regardless of filter
filter_matches() evaluated after stats_record() and before fputs; returns 1 if no filter active or syscall name in filter list
parse_filter() accepts strdup'd comma-separated name string and populates g_filter via strtok
-e trace=name,... option parsed by getopt with + prefix; filter_str passed to parse_filter
print_statistics() generates qsort-sorted table with columns: % time, seconds, usecs/call, calls, errors, syscall; -c flag triggers on exit
g_output FILE* defaults to stderr; -o filename redirects to fopen'd file; all output uses g_output
attach_to_process() for -p PID: ptrace(PTRACE_ATTACH) then waitpid(target_pid) then PTRACE_SETOPTIONS then pid_map_get
Mid-syscall attach handled: exit stop with outbuf_len==0 prints <... name resumed> = retval without calling stats_record
volatile sig_atomic_t g_interrupted set only in SIGINT handler; sigaction installed without SA_RESTART so waitpid returns EINTR
Main loop checks g_interrupted at top; calls detach_all() then breaks on interruption
detach_all() iterates pid_map slots; attempts PTRACE_DETACH directly; on ESRCH/EIO sends SIGSTOP + WNOHANG waitpid loop (100 attempts, 1ms sleep) before retry
Tracer exits cleanly after all processes exit or SIGINT with optional statistics table printed to g_output
